description_long: This object is a decorative potted plant featuring multiple pink daisy-like flowers with dark centers, each supported by slender, pale green stems. The flowers are arranged in a loose cluster, with some stems leaning outward and others standing more upright, creating a natural, slightly wild appearance. The pot is a tall, square-based container with a reddish-brown, marbled texture, suggesting it is made of ceramic or stone. The soil at the top of the pot is dark and appears slightly textured, with no visible leaves among the stems. The flowers are the most visually striking element, with their radial arrangement of petals and vibrant color contrasting against the earthy tones of the pot. This type of object is commonly found in urban environments such as sidewalks, building entrances, parks, or patios, serving as an ornamental feature.
description: A tall, marbled reddish-brown pot holds several pink daisy-like flowers with slender green stems and dark centers, arranged in a loose, natural cluster. The pot is filled with dark soil and lacks visible leaves.
description_view_0: Front view shows the marbled pot and a dense cluster of pink flowers, with several stems leaning outward.
description_view_1: This angle highlights more of the flower faces and reveals the uneven, natural spread of the stems.
description_view_2: The view emphasizes the soil surface and the arrangement of stems, with some flowers partially obscured.
description_view_3: This side shows a mix of upright and leaning stems, with the marbled pot clearly visible and several flower heads facing outward.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged radially around a central disc, forming a daisy-like composite structure. Each flower head displays a single whorl of petals with slight overlapping, creating a symmetrical and layered appearance.
